accepted.reason: Linear-algebra reconstruction pipeline with normalization and weighted projection is a strong numerical optimization target.
Datetime/date/time parsing and conversion helpers are practical data-processing kernels with clear performance pathways.
KNN distance-matrix construction and neighbor classification loops are directly relevant numeric kernels for Cython speedups.
Pixel plotting and line rasterization loops provide compact geometry kernels with predictable optimization opportunities.
FFT post-processing and duplicate-frequency aggregation combine vectorized and loop logic suitable for conversion tasks.
Monte Carlo accept/reject and adaptive-step simulation loops are high-value iterative kernels.

skipped.reason: Framework-bound OpenGL instruction wrappers are API glue, not portable compute kernels.
Brute-force string/print exploration script is pedagogical and not a clean optimization target for GRPO conversion tasks.
Mostly thin backend dispatch wrappers with limited standalone algorithmic content.
Highly project-specific integration with external game/NN interfaces; low portability as a standalone Python optimization example.
